behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in heuristic algorithm quality, inside one computational boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Integration rejected because behavior text is strictly about approximation ratio, which can be validated by feeding deterministic coordinate sets and comparing heuristic length vs exact optimal solver in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the heuristic function with many fixed/random stop sets (n<=20), compute exact optimal tour in-process (e.g., Held-Karp/brute-force for small n), assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is arithmetic/config-lookup logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Higher levels rejected because behavior text does not require real traffic API or external timing service; it only specifies computation from provided distances and configured speeds.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test estimator with table-driven cases: consecutive-stop distances + road types + speed config => expected segment durations/time windows, including rounding/boundary cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is threshold/excess calculation in validator logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Integration rejected because no module handoff is required by behavior text beyond passing numeric totals and shift hours.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test validator function with total_estimated_time and remaining_shift_hours pairs; assert accept/reject decision and exact excess output, including equality edge case and small-overrun precision case.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module boundaries (geocoder result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-report output). A unit test of only geocoder wrapper or only optimizer would miss a bug where failed addresses are dropped from routing but not included in skipped report (or vice versa). REAL-INFRA: no, in-process fake geocoder responses are sufficient; real HTTP not required for this behavior. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit would miss cross-module state mismatch between filtered stops and reported skipped addresses. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): not choosing above Integration because no concrete System/Agentic/Workflow-only failure is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test service with stub geocoding client returning coordinates for valid addresses and empty result for one address; assert output route excludes failed address, optimizer runs on remaining stops, and skipped-address list exactly contains the failed address.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: key risk is handoff correctness between deduplication/aggregation and route construction; a unit test of dedupe alone can pass while service still forwards duplicates to optimizer, causing double visit. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit would miss bug where consolidated data is produced but ignored by downstream route planner. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): no concrete System/Agentic/Workflow-only failure for this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test service input containing duplicate addresses with different item payloads; assert exactly one stop for that address enters planned route, items are combined correctly, and route output has no repeated visit for that location.
